meta.title: Evolutionary Mismatch
meta.teaser: Look at the hidden mismatch between ancient biological instincts and modern life, from food cravings to body image distress.

article.title: The Evolutionary Mismatch of Modern Diets

paragraphs.en: For most of human history, our early ancestors struggled to secure enough calories to survive. In order to occupy diverse and often harsh environments, humans evolved as adaptable omnivores and opportunistic carnivores. The primary biological ambition of our species was simple: avoid starvation. To ensure survival, evolution instilled in the human brain a profound desire for energy-dense foods—specifically those high in sugar, fat, and salt.
paragraphs.zh: 在人类历史的大部分时间里，我们早期的祖先为了生存而努力获取足够的卡路里。为了占据多样化且通常恶劣的环境，人类进化成了适应性强的杂食动物和机会主义的食肉动物。我们物种的主要生物学野心很简单：避免饥饿。为了确保生存，进化在人类大脑中灌输了对高能量食物的深刻渴望——特别是那些高糖、高脂肪和高盐的食物。

paragraphs.en: However, in the 21st century, the scope of our food environment has rapidly expanded. The modern agricultural and industrial revolution has created an environment of artificial abundance. Supermarkets are filled with highly processed products designed by food scientists to entice consumers. With vivid packaging and highly appealing tastes, these products intentionally trigger our ancient reward systems. While an occasional sweet treat may seem benign, the chronic over-intake of ultra-processed foods has become a modern health plague. This continuous dietary assault threatens to corrupt our natural biological hunger signals. When the body's delicate regulatory systems are overwhelmed, our metabolism can slip into a state of physiological anarchy, ultimately acting to destroy our long-term health.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，在21世纪，我们食物环境的范围迅速扩大。现代农业和工业革命创造了一个人造丰裕的环境。超市里充满了食品科学家设计的为了诱惑消费者的高度加工产品。凭借生动的包装和极具吸引力的口味，这些产品有意地触发了我们古老的奖励系统。虽然偶尔的甜食似乎是无害的，但超加工食品的慢性过度摄入已成为现代健康灾难。这种持续的饮食冲击威胁着要破坏我们自然的生物饥饿信号。当身体微妙的调节系统被压垮时，我们的新陈代谢就会滑入一种生理的无政府状态，最终破坏我们的长期健康。

paragraphs.en: In stark contrast to this environment of overconsumption, modern societies face a contradictory psychological crisis. Mass media and the fashion industry constantly convey unrealistic and hyper-thin standards of beauty. The public is often encouraged to adore digitally altered images of models, making many individuals feel doubtful about their own self-worth and body image. This societal pressure can promote extreme and unhealthy behaviors. Many people attempt to restore a sense of control over their bodies through a dutiful adherence to extreme fitness regimens or eccentric starvation diets.
paragraphs.zh: 与这种过度消费的环境形成鲜明对比的是，现代社会面临着一种矛盾的心理危机。大众媒体和时尚产业不断传达不切实际的和极度骨感的审美标准。公众经常被鼓励去敬慕经过数字修改的模特图像，这让许多人对自己的自我价值和身体形象感到怀疑。这种社会压力会促进极端和不健康的行为。许多人试图通过顺从地坚持极端健身计划或古怪的饥饿饮食来恢复对他们身体的控制感。

paragraphs.en: In severe cases, this psychological pressure manifests as clinical eating disorders, such as anorexia nervosa, a life-threatening psychiatric condition characterized by an intense fear of gaining weight. It is a tragic paradox that while we live in an era with a superior abundance of food, millions are starving themselves due to sociocultural pressures. This phenomenon suggests a backward step in our relationship with nourishment. To truly achieve well-being, we must recognize this evolutionary mismatch.
paragraphs.zh: 在严重的情况下，这种心理压力表现为临床饮食失调，如神经性厌食症，这是一种以强烈害怕体重增加为特征的危及生命的精神病学状况。这是一个可悲的悖论，虽然我们生活在一个食物极其丰富的时代，但数以百万计的人由于社会文化压力而正在挨饿。这种现象表明我们在与营养的关系上后退了一步。为了真正实现幸福，我们必须认识到这种进化上的错位。

questions.q: In the first paragraph, what does it mean that humans struggled to 'secure' enough calories?

options.text: They wanted to lock up their food in a safe place.

rationale.en: While 'secure' can mean making something safe physically, in this context, it means succeeding in obtaining something essential.
rationale.zh: 虽然‘secure’可以指在物理上使某物安全，但在这种语境下，它意味着成功获取某种必不可少的东西。

options.text: They succeeded in obtaining enough food to survive.

rationale.en: Here, 'secure' is used as a verb meaning to manage to get or obtain something (calories), especially when it is difficult.
rationale.zh: 在这里，‘secure’被用作动词，意思是设法得到或获取某物（卡路里），尤其是当这很困难的时候。

options.text: They tried to protect themselves from carnivores.

rationale.en: The sentence is explicitly talking about securing calories, not defending against predators.
rationale.zh: 这句话明确讨论的是获取卡路里，而不是防御捕食者。

options.text: They felt confident and safe about their diet.

rationale.en: The passage notes they 'struggled', meaning they definitely did not feel confident or safe regarding their food supply.
rationale.zh: 文章指出他们‘struggled’（挣扎，努力），这意味着他们对自己的食物供应绝对没有感到自信或安全。

questions.q: Why does the author refer to the chronic over-intake of processed foods as a 'plague'?

options.text: Because highly processed foods contain bacteria that cause infectious diseases.

rationale.en: The text does not mention bacteria; the damage is caused by the metabolic 'anarchy' from poor nutritional balance.
rationale.zh: 文本没有提到细菌；损害是由不良营养平衡引起的代谢‘anarchy’（混乱）造成的。

options.text: Because it acts as a disastrous, widespread health crisis affecting society.

rationale.en: The word 'plague' is used metaphorically to describe a devastating, widespread problem—in this case, the negative health impacts of modern diets.
rationale.zh: ‘plague’这个词被隐喻性地用来描述一个毁灭性的、广泛存在的问题——在这里是现代饮食对健康的负面影响。

options.text: Because it helps to corrupt biological hunger signals.

rationale.en: While it does corrupt hunger signals, the word 'plague' itself specifically emphasizes the widespread, disastrous nature of the consequence.
rationale.zh: 虽然它确实破坏了饥饿信号，但‘plague’这个词本身特别强调了后果的广泛性和灾难性。

options.text: Because it is an ancient disease that has been restored.

rationale.en: The text refers to it as a 'modern health plague', distinguishing it from ancient diseases.
rationale.zh: 文本将其称为‘现代健康灾难（plague）’，将其与古代疾病区分开来。

questions.q: Which word best serves as the opposite of 'benign' as used in the second paragraph?

options.text: Appealing

rationale.en: 'Appealing' means attractive. Something harmful can also be appealing (like sugary food).
rationale.zh: ‘Appealing’意味着有吸引力的。有害的东西也可能是有吸引力的（比如含糖食物）。

options.text: Eccentric

rationale.en: 'Eccentric' means weird or unconventional, not necessarily harmful or dangerous.
rationale.zh: ‘Eccentric’意味着古怪的或非传统的，不一定有害或危险。

options.text: Destructive

rationale.en: The text contrasts a 'benign' (harmless) occasional sweet treat with the 'destructive' long-term assault of an ultra-processed diet.
rationale.zh: 文本将‘benign’（无害的）偶尔甜食与超加工饮食的‘毁灭性’长期冲击进行了对比。

options.text: Superior

rationale.en: 'Superior' means better or higher in rank, not the opposite of harmless.
rationale.zh: ‘Superior’意味着更好或级别更高，而不是无害的反义词。

questions.q: According to the passage, why do some people adopt 'eccentric' starvation diets?

options.text: To expand the scope of their culinary experiences.

rationale.en: Starvation diets restrict culinary experiences; they don't expand them.
rationale.zh: 饥饿饮食限制了烹饪体验；它们没有扩大这些体验。

options.text: To try and restore a sense of control influenced by media beauty standards.

rationale.en: The text explains that mass media conveys unrealistic beauty standards, prompting people to use extreme (eccentric) diets to restore control over their body image.
rationale.zh: 文本解释说，大众媒体传达了不切实际的审美标准，促使人们使用极端（eccentric）饮食来恢复对身体形象的控制。

options.text: To instill a profound desire for energy-dense foods.

rationale.en: Evolution instilled that desire; eccentric diets are a modern reaction against weight gain.
rationale.zh: 进化灌输了这种渴望；古怪的饮食是现代对抗体重增加的反应。

options.text: Because they want to mimic the diet of opportunistic carnivores.

rationale.en: Carnivores are mentioned in the context of ancient evolution, not modern starvation diets.
rationale.zh: 食肉动物是在古代进化的语境中被提及的，而不是现代的饥饿饮食。

questions.q: What does the phrase 'slip into anarchy' imply about the body's metabolism in the modern food environment?

options.text: The metabolism completely shuts down and dies out.

rationale.en: It doesn't shut down; it loses its natural regulation.
rationale.zh: 它并没有停止运作；它失去了自然的调节。

options.text: The metabolism becomes perfectly efficient and superior.

rationale.en: This contradicts the negative tone of the passage regarding processed foods.
rationale.zh: 这与文章中关于加工食品的消极语调相矛盾。

options.text: The metabolic system loses its natural order and regulation.

rationale.en: 'Anarchy' means a state of disorder or lack of a governing system. In this context, the body's regulatory systems are overwhelmed and stop functioning correctly.
rationale.zh: ‘Anarchy’意味着混乱状态或缺乏管理系统。在这种语境下，身体的调节系统被压垮并停止正常运作。

options.text: The metabolism attempts a physical assault on the brain.

rationale.en: This is a misinterpretation of the words 'assault' and 'anarchy' used in the text.
rationale.zh: 这是对文本中使用的‘assault’和‘anarchy’这两个词的误解。
